name: Dequantization
oneliner: Reverse mapping from low-precision quantized values back to approximate floats — happens on-the-fly during inference.
explanation: Dequantization is the process of converting quantized weights back to a higher-precision format during inference so they can participate in standard matrix multiplication. In a 4-bit weight scheme, integer weights are read from memory at 4 bits, multiplied by their group scale factor, and fed directly into 16-bit matrix math. This is fused with the computation so the full-precision weight matrix is never materialized in memory, meaning only 4 bits per weight travel across the memory bus. The dequantization cost is negligible compared to the matrix multiply itself.
fundamentals: Integer: W_approx = scale $\times$ (W_q - zero_point). Error: |W_original - W_approx| ≤ scale/2. nf4: W_approx = nf4_codebook[index] $\times$ absmax. W8A8 output: Y_fp16 = Y_int32 $\times$ (s_x $\times$ s_w).
